toolbar.fuzzyTitle: Fuzzy-match commits by how similar their actual file changes are (used when SHA / title / patch-id all fail to match). Scored by the overlap ratio of changed lines, and subsets still match: when a TOT branch changes several projects together, a personal branch that touches only one of them can still be linked.
toolbar.swapTitle: Swap the left and right sides (L ⇄ R)
toolbar.loadAllTitle: Load the full commit history up to the Settings "Commits to load" limit. On open, only the pre-load count is shown for a fast start.
toolbar.undoTitle: Undo the last note / color / virtual-tag / manual-link edit (Ctrl+Z)
toolbar.redoTitle: Redo the last undone edit (Ctrl+Y / Ctrl+Shift+Z)
toolbar.clearManualTitle: Delete all manual links and clear the cache
toolbar.clearNotesTitle: Delete all notes and clear the cache
toolbar.exportTitle: Open export options for Excel or Markdown review reports
toolbar.logTitle: Open the error / diagnostics log (git failures, cache problems, export errors)

log.title: 🧾 Error / Log
log.copyTitle: Copy the whole log to the clipboard
log.empty: No log entries yet. Git failures, cache problems and export errors will appear here.

settings.title: ⚙ Settings
settings.languageHint: Choose the interface language. Your choice is saved automatically.
settings.themeHint: Choose the colour theme. Your choice is saved automatically.
settings.commitLimitHint: How many commits to load per repo (0 = no limit). Default is 2,000. Applies on the next repo load or refresh.
settings.preloadHint: How many commits to load when a repo first opens, for a fast start. Default is 250. Click "Load all logs" to load the full "Commits to load" amount.
settings.autoFillHint: When the two sides stop at different points in history, automatically load the shallower side deeper to realign matched commits hidden by truncation (0 = off). Also caps how many commits each side may auto-load per pass. Default is 100.

help.title: ⌨ Keyboard Shortcuts
shortcuts: Open the folder picker (fills the left / right repo in order)
Jump to the next search result
Jump to the previous search result
Move to the previous commit in the same column
Move to the next commit in the same column
Move focus to the closest commit in the left column
Move focus to the closest commit in the right column
Open the detail window for the current commit
Open that commit's detail window (multiple can be open at once)
Pick this commit to compare (pick two, then Compare)
Close all popups / clear selection / cancel linking
Delete the currently selected manual link
In the note window: save and close
Undo the last note / color / virtual-tag / manual-link edit

gitBar.switchBranchTitle:
Switch branch
Lists every branch as a tree; pick one to switch.
gitBar.branchMapTitle:
Worktree
Manage worktrees (add / remove) and every branch: search, switch, and update all from origin.
gitBar.fetchTitle:
git fetch --all --prune
Updates only the remote-tracking refs (origin/*); it does not change your local commits.
The view usually stays the same — use Pull to apply updates.
gitBar.loadMoreTitle:
Load more / align
When the two sides are misaligned, the first click lines them back up (loads the shallower side down to the other side's depth). Each further click then loads more on both sides together.
gitBar.commitsTruncatedTitle: Commits loaded (truncated — older history not yet loaded)

branchMap.title: Worktree
branchMap.pickHint: Select a branch to switch to it or add a worktree.
branchMap.switchTitle: Switch the working tree to the selected branch (git switch).
branchMap.addWorktreeTitle: Create a new git worktree for the selected branch in a folder you choose.
branchMap.updateTitle:
git fetch --all --prune, then fast-forward every local branch to its origin upstream.
Diverged branches are left untouched.
branchMap.mainWtTitle: The main / current worktree can't be removed.
branchMap.removeTitle: Remove this worktree (git worktree remove --force) and delete its folder.
branchMap.updateSubmodulesTitle: git submodule update --init --recursive inside this worktree. Objects are borrowed from the mirror cache first, then this repo, else the network. Each submodule is shown as [mirror], [local-cache], or [network].
branchMap.copyTitle: Copy the full log to the clipboard
branchMap.gitCmdsTitle: Handy git commands — click one to copy it
branchMap.gitCmdsHint: Click a command to copy it, then paste into a terminal opened at the folder.
branchMap.copyCmdHint: Copy this command to the clipboard

cmd.mergeMain: Merge updated main into your branch

cmdTip.subUpdate: Init and update ALL submodules recursively, 8 in parallel with a progress bar. Use for the first checkout or after a big submodule bump.
cmdTip.subUpdateShallow: Same as above but fetches only the latest commit per submodule (--depth 1): smallest, fastest download. No full history; if a pinned commit isn't at a branch tip it can fail — then drop --depth 1.
cmdTip.subSync: Re-copy submodule URLs from .gitmodules into .git/config, recursively. Run this first when a submodule's remote URL has changed.
cmdTip.subStatus: List every submodule's checked-out commit, recursively. Leading '-' = not initialized, '+' = commit differs from the superproject record, blank = in sync.
cmdTip.status: Show the current branch plus staged, unstaged and untracked changes. The safe thing to check before you do anything.
cmdTip.fetch: Download the latest refs from ALL remotes and delete local remote-tracking branches that no longer exist upstream (--prune). Updates refs only — your working tree is untouched.
cmdTip.pullRebase: Fetch, then replay YOUR local commits on top of the updated upstream (rebase instead of merge) for a clean, linear history. Commit or stash local changes first.
cmdTip.mergeMain: Merge the locally-updated 'main' into the branch checked out in this worktree. On conflicts git stops so you can resolve them, then 'git commit'.
cmdTip.log: Show the last 20 commits, one line each, with an ASCII graph and branch/tag labels — a quick look at branch and merge topology.
cmdTip.branchVV: List all local branches with their tracked upstream and how many commits each is ahead/behind — handy to see sync state at a glance.
cmdTip.resetHard: Force the current branch back to HEAD and throw away ALL uncommitted changes. DESTRUCTIVE: staged and working-tree edits are gone for good. Only when you're sure.
cmdTip.clean: Delete every untracked file and folder (-x also removes gitignored, -f force, -d include directories). DESTRUCTIVE: preview first with 'git clean -xfdn'.

branchMap.createMirrorTitle: Build a local cache of bare mirrors for every (nested) submodule in a folder you pick — seeded from this repo (fast) or the network. Worktree submodule updates then pull from the cache. Uses extra disk.
branchMap.mirrorDone: Mirror cache built: {count} submodule mirror(s) at {path}
branchMap.lockedByHead: This folder is in use by:
branchMap.lockedByHint: Close the program(s) above (or end them in Task Manager), then remove the worktree again.
branchMap.lockedUnknown: The folder is locked, but the holding process couldn't be identified. Check an open editor, a terminal whose current directory is inside it, or antivirus/indexing, then try again.
branchMap.refreshTitle: Reload the branch and worktree lists

worktree.title: Create git worktree
worktree.newBranchLocal: New branch (optional — blank checks out the branch)
worktree.newBranchCommit: New branch (optional — blank uses a detached HEAD)
worktree.createTitle: Run git worktree add at the chosen folder.
worktree.createMirrorTitle: Create a local bare mirror clone (git clone --mirror) of this repo in the chosen folder.

export.head: ⬇ Export
export.excelDesc: Aligned rows with colors, note comments, hyperlinks, and manual-link sheet.
export.markdownDesc: Table-heavy summary for sharing unique commits, fuzzy risk, manual links, and notes.
export.question: {total} rows are available to export. How many do you want to write?
export.warn: ⚠ The dataset is large; exporting may take a while or produce a large file.

vtag.title: 🏷️ Virtual tag
vtag.placeholder: Type a version label…  (Enter to save)

detail.codeTitle: Show the code this commit changed (added / removed lines), like the compare view

Open pull request #{n} in the browser

Search code for {n} in the browser

detail.relatedBodyTitle: Click to view the matching commit details
detail.noRelated: No matching item (this commit only exists on {side})

detail.toastVSCodeNotFound: VS Code not found. Please install it and make sure the `code` command is on your PATH.

code.title: </> Code changes
code.filesTitle: Number of files this commit changed
code.empty: This commit changed no files (empty or merge commit).

compare.title: Side-by-side compare
compare.simTitle: Jaccard overlap of the two commits' changed lines
compare.empty: Neither commit changed any files (empty or merge commit).
compare.pillTitle: Open a side-by-side line diff of the two linked commits
compare.pillAria: Compare the two linked commits side by side
compare.goTitle: Open a side-by-side line diff of the two picked commits

row.nodeDisconnect: Click to disconnect this manual link
row.nodePick: Pick a node on the other side to link · Esc to cancel
row.nodeStart: Click to start a manual link

app.aligningSub: Loading the shallower side down to the same depth so matched commits line up. This can take a moment for a large gap.
app.pagingMoreSub: Pulling in the next batch of older commits on both sides.
app.noRepoSub: Pick the left and right Git folders from the toolbar above to start comparing commit history
app.noMatchSub: Adjust your keywords, or turn off "matches only" to show everything
app.noCommitSub: This repository has no history to display
app.hintLinking: Pick a node on the other side to link · Esc to cancel
app.hintDefault: Click a row node ◗ to link two commits · Del removes a selected manual link
